data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0047-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground orientation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session. Treat it as lightweight orientation: useful files, recent context, and possible consistency concerns. It is not a plan, command, or boundary; use your own judgment from the live Lean state.
# Orientation — opening Ground, run 0013 (workspace-wide, no prior Horizon diff)

- **Active high-priority leaves, current accurate inventory** (grepped this session, tighter than some mid-T12 memory): `AJC.fbc` has exactly 3 tactic sorries in `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` — `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (`:197`) and the two cosimplicial naturality fields (`:1690`, `:1758`); attack layer in memory `I-0083`/`I-0076`. `FlatBaseChange.lean` is sorry-free.

- **`AJC.picrep` Quot endgame:** `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotFunctorDef.lean` now carries 3 sorries — `Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback` (`:340`), `hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap` (`:358`), and the headline `QuotScheme` (`:753`); `Grassmannian.representable` and the two flatness/finite leaves closed since `I-0113` was written. The separate `QuotScheme.lean` retains 3 FBC-gated base-change leaves (`:5079/:5130/:5180`). Deep remaining Hilbert input is Serre finite-generation `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` (`I-0109`).

- **Known statement defect on the Quot headline (`I-0118`):** the pinned `Scheme.QuotScheme` (`QuotFunctorDef.lean:753`) hypothesizes only `[IsProper] [LocallyOfFiniteType]` over arbitrary quasi-coherent `L,E`, weaker/false vs Nitsure §5 (projective π, relatively very ample `L`, coherent `E`). Blueprint node `thm:quot_representable` (`blueprint/.../Picard_QuotScheme.tex:4402`) is `\notready` with an inline mismatch note. Faithful restatement is blocked on projectivity/very-ampleness vocabulary absent from Mathlib v4.31 (`I-0109` ampleness gap).

- **Other live spines:** `AJC.pic0av` `tangentSpaceIso` path is unblocked post-FGA-rewire — Kleiman §5 H¹ comparison through `(representable C).homEquiv` at `Spec k[ε]`, ring-level truncated-exponential split already landed (`DualNumberUnits.lean`); see `I-0061`/`I-0082`. `ALB.codim1` is gated on the single Milne 3.3 leaf `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (`I-0065`, `CodimOneExtension.lean`), which then unblocks `ALB.up`.

# Task focus
Task: T9
Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean,blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese*.tex,blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex

If representability is not yet proven, don't try to fill its sorries, this is another task, just work on the albanese part. For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve $C/k$ with $k$-rational point, the Jacobian $J = \mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}$ satisfies the Albanese universal property
$$\forall\, (A,a)\ \text{pointed abelian variety},\quad \mathrm{Hom}_{\ast}(C,A)\ \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\ \mathrm{Hom}_{\mathrm{AbVar}}(J,A),$$
and is the unique such object up to canonical isomorphism.

You may read the Albanese subproject to see whether there has already been progress not updated with this project.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC — axiom-clean. The engine cone is sorry-free on Lean v4.31 (the former migration-interim sorries closed by T8). Residual base-change sorries live in `AJC.fbc`, not here.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **02KG affine-reduction heart closed**: the per-$\sigma$ heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (ring-pushout carve + corner-sections tensor rewrite + the affine brick `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`), together with `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), the RHS tilde leaf `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`, and arbitrary-morphism pullback quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, 01BG) are sorry-free and axiom-clean. The superseded canonical-mate route was pruned; `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` is now sorry-free. Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **3 leaf sorries** (all in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`): the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`, `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2** and memory `I-0083`.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
(empty)

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, `## Why I stopped`, and `## Next`; add or rename other sections if another shape is clearer, but keep `## Progress` and `## Why I stopped`. In `## Progress`, use only inline `-` bullets, one per relevant file or target, e.g. `- FileA.lean: 4 sorries -> 3 sorries; closed the base case.` or `- FileB.lean: No change because dependency X is blocked.` Do not use nested bullets or wrapped multi-line bullets there. In `## Why I stopped`, start with either `Task complete:` when the assigned objective is genuinely complete, or `Task not complete:` when work remains. If there is a plausible next action inside the session's scope and budget, take it before stopping; a clean commit or green partial check is not by itself a reason to stop. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this {role} session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

=== T9 memory ===
---
name: t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map
description: "T9 Albanese endgame after the port landed — the two greenfield sub-builds that gate it, the sole extension-leg soundness sorry, the function-field pullback bridge (K(Y)→K(X) NOW COMPLETE), and blueprint fixes (run 0013 T9)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 7d972899-ebe5-42b1-a8d3-5d962e252ce0
---

**T9 Albanese endgame: the rational-map-extension port LANDED + verified; both remaining legs are gated on large greenfield sub-builds, not one-session work. (run 0013 T9, session 0017.)**

- **Port done + verified.** All 8 `AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*.lean` files are in AJC, byte-identical to the `Albanese` subproject, wired in the root, and `lake build` green (8573 jobs). CodimOne 3→1, Thm32 2→0 in-file sorries. Support files `PolePurity`/`SmoothPrimeRegularity`/`StandardSmoothDimension` are **axiom-clean** (kernel-verified `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`). The port was already captured in the run-0013 baseline snapshot `3029a02d` — `horizon commit` on it is a no-op (nothing to commit). So [[pullback-tilde-01hq-closed]]-style "port before assembling" (inbox I-0112) is now COMPLETE.

- **Extension leg soundness = ONE sorry.** `extend_to_av` (Thm32, sorry-free in-file) and `albanese_universal_property` (a sorry-free *assembly*) both carry `sorryAx` transitively via the single sorry `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3, `CodimOneExtension.lean:1662`). It needs 4 substeps: (1) difference map `Φ:X×X⇢G` via the `GrpObj` group law + product-of-rational-maps (needs X×X integral — why the thm carries `GeometricallyIrreducible`); (2) slice `(x,x)∈Dom Φ ↔ x∈Dom f`; (3) function-field pullback; (4b) diagonal codim-1 Krull bound (4a already closed in `PolePurity.lean`). Substeps 1,2,4b are independent greenfield gaps — NOT one session. Do not expect the bridge below to close this.

- **UP-assembly leg blocked on the symmetric-power scheme substrate.** Mathlib v4.31 has **no** scheme-level `Sym^g`, no quotient of a scheme by a finite group action, no GIT/geometric quotient, no Noether finiteness of invariants (only type/module `Sym`, `FixedPoints.subalgebra/subring/subfield`, `Algebra.IsInvariant` for Galois). All of `AlbaneseUP.lean`'s `abelJacobi`/`SymmetricPower`/`symmetricPowerAVMap`/`symmetricPowerToJacobian`/`descentThroughBirationalSigma`/`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` are downstream. Building `Sym^g C` (Milne III.3, glue `Spec((A^{⊗g})^{S_g})`) is a large multi-session sub-build. `bundle` (A.3 representability) is out of T9 scope. `albanese_universal_property` is ALREADY sorry-free assembly from the two theorem sorries.

- **Bridge substrate COMPLETE (infrastructure, closes NO sorry). (run 0013 session 0022.)** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.{stalkPullback, fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint, functionFieldPullback}` (`Albanese/RationalMapFunctionField.lean`, all axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, committed `0db13b411b`, blueprint-pinned `lem:fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` + `def:functionFieldPullback` `3805df7906`). `stalkPullback` = germ pullback `𝒪_{Y,f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)`; the **dominant refinement** is now DONE: for `[IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y] (f : X ⤏ Y) [f.IsDominant]`, `fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` proves `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`, so `functionFieldPullback : Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField` via `Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (Inseparable.of_eq …) .hom ≫ f.stalkPullback`. The two flagged "walls" were NOT walls: `haveI : Nonempty ↥g.domain := ⟨⟨genericPoint X, hη⟩⟩` where `hη := (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec`, then `IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance` (mathlib `[IsIntegral X][Nonempty U]→IsIntegral U`) gives `IrreducibleSpace` free. Recipe: `obtain ⟨g,rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep` + `(PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›`; `rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]`; `have : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη ≫ g.hom := rfl` then `rw [·, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]`; inner point via `(g.domain.ι).isOpenEmbedding.injective` + `genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion` + `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`; dominant image via `(genericPoint_spec _).image g.hom.continuous` + `Set.image_univ` + `g.hom.denseRange.closure_range` + `IsGenericPoint.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)`. Skeptical note: `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` is UNPINNED, so the field hom closes nothing directly — it enables a future `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`.
- **NEXT-FRONTIER FACT (why Milne 3.3 substep-1 is a multi-brick greenfield): mathlib's `RationalMap` API (Birational/RationalMap.lean) has ONLY `compHom` (compose a rational map with a morphism on the RIGHT).** No rational-map products, no general composition, no precomposition with a dominant/open morphism. So the difference map `Φ = m∘(f×f) : X×X ⇢ G` needs new project-side primitives: (a) precompose a rational map with the projections `pr_i : X×X → X` (an open dominant morphism — needs "preimage of dense open under open map is dense"), and (b) a pairing `X×X ⇢ G×G` into a product target. THEN substeps 2/3/4b. `functionFieldPullback` supplies only the substep-3 K(Y)→K(X) piece for a *dominant* map (Milne's Φ pulls back `𝒪_{G,e}` at `Φ(x,x)=e`, a germ-at-a-point criterion, not the generic-point field hom) — so even substep 3 needs a germ-definedness criterion, not this. Net: closing `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` is ≥ a multi-session infra build.

1	# Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy of a rational map into a group variety
2	
3	Reference for `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
4	(`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean:1692`). This is the **single remaining sorry** gating the whole
5	Albanese rational-map-extension chain (Milne Theorem 3.2 → `extend_to_av` →
6	Albanese universal property). Blueprint node: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`
7	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`).
8	
9	## Milne's statement (Abelian Varieties, §I.3 Lemma 3.3, p. 17)
10	
11	> Let `φ : V ⇢ G` be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
12	> `φ` is defined on all of `V`, or the points where it is not defined form a closed subset of
13	> pure codimension 1 in `V` (i.e., a finite union of prime divisors).
14	
15	Combined with Milne 3.1 (`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`, codim ≥ 2 into a complete
16	target — **proved**), for an abelian-variety target this forces `Z(φ) = ∅`
17	(`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`), hence extension (Milne 3.2).
18	
19	## Milne's proof (transcribed verbatim from the PDF, pages 23–24)
20	
21	Define a rational map `Φ : V × V ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ φ(x)·φ(y)⁻¹`. More precisely, if `(U, φ_U)`
22	represents `φ`, then `Φ` is represented by
23	```
24	U × U --(φ_U × φ_U)--> G × G --(id × inv)--> G × G --m--> G.
25	```
26	Clearly `Φ` is defined at a diagonal point `(x, x)` if `φ` is defined at `x`, and then
27	`Φ(x, x) = e`. Conversely, if `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`, then it is defined on an open
28	neighbourhood of `(x, x)`; in particular there is an open `U ⊆ V` such that `Φ` is defined on
29	`{x} × U`. After possibly replacing `U` by a smaller open subset (not necessarily containing `x`),
30	`φ` will be defined on `U`. For `u ∈ U`, the formula `φ(x) = Φ(x, u)·φ(u)` defines `φ` at `x`.
31	Thus **`φ` is defined at `x` iff `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`.**
32	
33	The rational map `Φ` defines a map `Φ* : 𝒪_{G,e} → k(V × V)`. Since `Φ` sends `(x, x)` to `e` if
34	it is defined there, `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)` **iff `Im(𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{V×V,(x,x)}`.**
35	
36	Now `V × V` is nonsingular, so there is a good theory of divisors. For a nonzero rational function
37	`f` on `V × V`, write `div(f) = div(f)₀ − div(f)_∞` with both effective (`div(f)_∞ = div(f⁻¹)₀`).
38	Then
39	```
40	𝒪_{V×V,(x,x)} = { f ∈ k(V×V) | div(f)_∞ does not contain (x,x) } ∪ {0}.
41	```
42	Suppose `φ` is not defined at `x`. Then for some `f ∈ Im(φ*)`, `(x, x) ∈ div(f)_∞`, and clearly
43	`Φ` is not defined at the points `(y, y) ∈ Δ ∩ div(f)_∞`. **This is a subset of pure codimension
44	one in `Δ` (AG 9.2)**, and when we identify it with a subset of `V`, it is a subset of `V` of
45	codimension one passing through `x` on which `φ` is not defined. ∎
46	
47	## The 4-substep Lean decomposition
48	
49	Let `X = V` (smooth integral over `k̄`), `G` a smooth group variety, `f : X ⇢ G` over `k̄`.
50	`Φ = differenceRationalMap f hover : X ×_{k̄} X ⇢ G` (built in `Albanese/DifferenceMap.lean`).
51	
52	- **Sub-step 1 — the difference map `Φ`.** DONE (`differenceRationalMap`, `GrpObj.diff`,
53	  `grpObjDiffLeft`, `differenceRationalMap_compHom_over`). Axiom-clean.
54	- **Sub-step 1 domain bound / Sub-step 2 easy direction — `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f) ⊆ Dom(Φ)`.**
55	  DONE (session 0042, `le_domain_differenceRationalMap` in `DifferenceMap.lean`, axiom-clean).
56	  This is Milne's "clearly `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)` if `φ` is defined at `x`". Supporting:
57	  `le_domain_compHom` (compHom domain monotonicity — a Mathlib gap), `precompDiffPairing` (the
58	  explicit `U×U → G×G` morphism), `precompDiffPairing_toRationalMap` (it represents `prod`).
59	  Blueprint: `lem:difference_map_domain_lower_bound`, `lem:compHom_domain_monotone`.
60	- **Sub-step 2 hard direction — `Φ` defined at `(x,x)` ⟹ `φ` defined at `x`.** OPEN. Milne's
61	  group-law slice: pick `a ∈ Dom(f)` with `(x, a) ∈ Dom(Φ)` (openness of `Dom(Φ)` ∋ `(x,x)` +
62	  density of `Dom(f)`), then `φ(·) = Φ(·, a)·φ(a)` is regular at `x`. Needs: the slice map
63	  `Φ(·, a)`, right-translation by the fixed element `φ(a)`, and the rational-map identity.
64	- **Sub-step 3 — definedness ⟺ `Φ*(𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X,(x,x)}`.** OPEN. Anchors the pullback at
65	  `e ∈ G` (Φ maps the diagonal to `e`, giving an affine chart around `e`). `functionFieldPullback`
66	  (`RationalMapFunctionField.lean`, K(G) → K(X×X) for dominant Φ) and `stalkPullback` exist, but
67	  the germ pullback at `e` (not at `Φ(η)`) needs the closure-of-image `Ḡ ∋ e` OR a direct
68	  local-ring pullback along `Φ`. **Dominance caveat:** `Φ` need not be dominant onto `G` (if
69	  `im(f)` lands in a proper subgroup); replace `G` by the closure of the image, a subgroup.
70	- **Sub-step 4a — pole purity on `X×X`.** DONE (`Albanese/PolePurity.lean`,
71	  `Scheme.exists_specializes_coheight_eq_one_of_notMem_stalk_range`): `h ∈ K(X×X)` not regular at
72	  `P` ⟹ ∃ coheight-1 `z ⤳ P` with `h` not regular at `z`. Axiom-clean.
73	- **Sub-step 4b — `Δ ∩ div(f)_∞` is pure codim 1 in `Δ ≅ X`.** OPEN. The pole divisor of
74	  `f = Φ*(g)` on `X×X` meets the diagonal `Δ` (which is NOT contained in it, since `Φ ≡ e` on the
75	  generic diagonal point, so `f = g(e)` is regular there generically) in pure codim 1 — Krull's
76	  Hauptidealsatz / a local equation of `div(f)_∞` restricted to `Δ`. This transports a codim-1
77	  pole point of `X×X` through `Δ` to a coheight-1 `z ∈ X` with `x ∈ closure{z}` and `z ∈ Z(f)`.
78	  **Note:** `f|_Δ` is the constant `g(e)`, so this does NOT reduce to pole-purity-on-`X` of a
79	  single function; it genuinely needs the `X×X` divisor + local-equation-restricted-to-`Δ`.
80	
81	## Assembly (once 2-hard, 3, 4b land)
82	
83	Given `x₀ ∈ Z(f)`: by (2-hard) `(x₀,x₀) ∈ Z(Φ)`; by (3) some `g ∈ 𝒪_{G,e}` has `Φ*(g) ∉
84	𝒪_{X×X,(x₀,x₀)}`; by (4a) a codim-1 pole point `w ⤳ (x₀,x₀)`; by (4b) transport through `Δ` to
85	codim-1 `z ∈ Z(f)` with `x₀ ∈ closure{z}`. The `z ∈ Z(f)` conjunct is essential (memory
86	`t9-albanese-endgame-unblock-map`): without it the disjunct is vacuous and cannot combine with
87	Milne 3.1. **Do NOT reintroduce** `extend_of_codimOneFree` (false: `ℙ² ⇢ ℙ¹`).
88	
89	## Lean engineering recipes discovered (session 0042)
90	
91	- **Over-ness of `f.toPartialMap`**: register local `Scheme.Over` instances `⟨X.hom⟩`, `⟨G.hom⟩`,
92	  then `f.IsOver S := RationalMap.isOver_iff.mpr hover`; the `f.toPartialMap.IsOver S` instance
93	  fires (needs `[IsReduced X.left] [G.left.IsSeparated]`), and `PartialMap.isOver_iff.mp
94	  inferInstance` gives `f.toPartialMap.hom ≫ G.hom = f.toPartialMap.domain.ι ≫ X.hom`. The `↘`
95	  reduces to `X.hom`/`G.hom` by defeq (`exact h`, not `simpa`).
96	- **Pullback projections are both over `S`**: register `(pullback X.hom X.hom).Over S :=
97	  ⟨pullback.fst _ _ ≫ X.hom⟩`; then `fst.IsOver S := ⟨rfl⟩`, `snd.IsOver S :=
98	  ⟨pullback.condition.symm⟩`. So both `f.toPartialMap.precomp prᵢ` inherit over-ness via
99	  `precomp_isOver` — avoids unfolding `precomp_hom` (which hits an instance-transparency wall).
100	- **KERNEL-TIMEOUT GOTCHA**: building the pairing partial map inline with `set aR/bR/Ψ := …` +
101	  `pullback.lift` causes `(kernel) deterministic timeout` (nested `set`-lets over pullback/scheme
102	  terms). FIX: extract the pairing as a **top-level `noncomputable def`** (`precompDiffPairing`,
103	  `where`-syntax, no `set`); it kernel-checks once in isolation and the consumer references it
104	  opaquely. (Consistent with the standing `set`-is-problematic lesson.)
105	- **Function-field match** (`precompDiffPairing_toRationalMap`): `eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq` +
106	  `prod_fromFunctionField` + `pullback.hom_ext`; per leg, rewrite RHS `(f.precomp prᵢ).ff =
107	  (f.toPartialMap.precomp prᵢ).ff` (`← fromFunctionField_toRationalMap`, `precomp_toRationalMap`,
108	  `toRationalMap_toPartialMap`), then `← fromFunctionField_restrict … dense_domain inf_le_left`,
109	  `simp only [PartialMap.fromFunctionField, fromSpecStalkOfMem, restrict_hom, Category.assoc]`,
110	  `congr 1` (proof-irrelevant stalk map), `exact pullback.lift_fst _ _ _`.
111	- `set_option`/`omit … in` must precede the docstring, not sit between it and the theorem.
112

1620	`f.toPartialMap` is defined on all of `X`, and its composite with the
1621	isomorphism `X ≅ (⊤ : X.Opens)` is the required morphism. Uniqueness is the
1622	reduced-and-separated agreement principle (`hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq`).
1623	
1624	**Statement-correction note (run-0006 T6, session 0015).** This lemma
1625	*replaces* the former `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`, whose statement —
1626	"`CodimOneFree f` alone implies a regular extension, for an arbitrary
1627	complete target" — was **false as stated**: the projection `ℙ² ⇢ ℙ¹` away
1628	from a point satisfies every hypothesis (smooth integral source, complete
1629	smooth integral target, indeterminacy a single codim-2 closed point, hence
1630	`CodimOneFree`), yet admits no regular extension (any extension `g` would
1631	force `⊤ = g.toPartialMap.domain ≤ f.domain` via
1632	`PartialMap.le_domain_toRationalMap`, but `f.domain = ℙ² ∖ {pt}`). Milne 3.1
1633	asserts only the codim-≥2 conclusion; the everywhere-extension in Milne 3.2
1634	needs the *group-variety* input of Lemma 3.3 to force `Z(f) = ∅` first.

1638	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} [IsReduced X] [Y.IsSeparated]
1639	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) :
1640	    ∃! (g : X ⟶ Y), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1641	  -- The domain of definition is all of `X`.
1642	  have hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤ := by
1643	    rw [toPartialMap_domain, ← TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inj,
1644	      TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, ← Set.compl_empty_iff]
1645	    exact h
1646	  -- The candidate morphism: transport the maximal representative along
1647	  -- `X ≅ ⊤ ≅ f.toPartialMap.domain`.
1648	  have hwit : (X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge
1649	      ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom).toRationalMap = f := by
1650	    conv_rhs => rw [← f.toRationalMap_toPartialMap]
1651	    refine PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ?_
1652	    refine ⟨⊤, by rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top]; exact dense_univ,
1653	      le_top, hdom.ge, ?_⟩
1654	    simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap,
1655	      Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc,
1656	      Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]
1657	  exact ⟨_, hwit, fun g hg => hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq (hg.trans hwit.symm)⟩

1659	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1660	
1661	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1662	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1663	`thm:indeterminacy_codimGe2`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus
1664	to be empty when the target is an abelian variety, at which point
1665	`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` extends.
1666	
1667	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1668	
1669	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1670	codim 1.**
1671	
1672	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1673	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1674	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1675	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or of *pure codimension 1*,
1676	encoded as: every point of `Z(f)` lies in the closure of a codim-1 point
1677	*of `Z(f)` itself*. (The `z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f` conjunct is essential:
1678	without it the disjunct is nearly vacuous — on a variety every non-generic
1679	point specialises from *some* codim-1 point — and, in particular, too weak
1680	to combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 to force `Z(f) = ∅`
1681	in Theorem 3.2. Statement strengthened in run-0006 T6, session 0015.)
1682	
1683	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1684	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1685	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1686	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1687	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1688	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1689	
1690	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1691	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1692	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1693	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1694	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1695	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1696	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1697	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1698	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1699	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1700	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1701	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1702	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
1703	    (_hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
1704	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1705	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1706	        ∃ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1707	          Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1708	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1709	  -- full transcribed proof, substep DAG, and current status. Each substep is
1710	  -- project-side buildable on top of Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the
1711	  -- group-object API; substep 4b (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal
1712	  -- is pure codim-1) is the substantive Mathlib gap.
1713	  --
1714	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1715	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`. **DONE**
1716	  --   (`Albanese/DifferenceMap.lean`, `differenceRationalMap`); its domain
1717	  --   contains `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f)` (**DONE**, session 0042,
1718	  --   `le_domain_differenceRationalMap`), which is the easy direction of
1719	  --   substep 2 (`Φ` defined at `(x,x)` if `f` defined at `x`).
1720	  -- * Substep 2 (hard direction): `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ⟹ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the
1721	  --   formula `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1722	  --   neighbourhood — from the group-law + openness of `Dom(Φ)` and density
1723	  --   of `Dom(f)`. **OPEN.**
1724	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1725	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1726	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1727	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1728	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1729	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1730	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1731	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1732	  --   **Substep 4a (pole-divisor purity) is PROVED (run-0006 T6, session
1733	  --   0019)**: `Albanese/PolePurity.lean` provides
1734	  --   `Scheme.exists_specializes_coheight_eq_one_of_notMem_stalk_range` —
1735	  --   on a locally Noetherian integral scheme with regular stalks (e.g.
1736	  --   `X × X` smooth over `k̄`, via `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
1737	  --   applied to the product), an `h ∈ K(X×X)` not regular at a point `Q₀`
1738	  --   fails to be regular at some coheight-1 generization `z ⤳ Q₀`. The
1739	  --   proof is Serre-free/UFD-free (elementary swap-pair argument replaces
1740	  --   normality; blueprint node `lem:pole_divisor_purity`, axiom-clean).
1741	  --   **Substep 4b (diagonal intersection)** remains: transport the
1742	  --   coheight-1 pole point of `X × X` through `Δ` to a coheight-1 point
1743	  --   of `X` inside `Z(f)` (Krull height bound for the diagonal, a local
1744	  --   complete intersection of codim `dim X` since `X` is smooth).
1745	  --
1746	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1747	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (Substeps 1–3: difference map, slice argument,
1748	  -- `Φ^*` of `𝒪_{G,e}`; also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1749	  -- (b) Substep 4b, the codim-1 diagonal-intersection bound (Hartshorne
1750	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form). Substep 4a is closed by
1751	  -- `Albanese/PolePurity.lean` (imported above).
1752	  sorry

1754	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1755	
1756	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1757	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1758	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1759	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1760	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1761	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1762	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1763

1767	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapPrecomp
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapProd
9	
10	/-!
11	# Milne's difference rational map `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`
12	
13	This file assembles **Sub-step 1** of Milne's Lemma 3.3
14	(`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
15	from a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over an algebraically closed
16	field `k̄`, build the *difference rational map*
17	
18	  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`,   `Φ(x, y) = f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`,
19	
20	which is the vehicle for reducing the indeterminacy of `f` to a pole-divisor
21	computation on the nonsingular surface `X ×_{k̄} X`.
22	
23	The construction glues the two rational-map bricks already in the project:
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` (`Albanese/RationalMapPrecomp.lean`) — precompose
26	  `f` with the two projections `prᵢ : X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X`, which are open because
27	  `X.hom` is smooth (hence its base change `prᵢ` is smooth, flat, locally of finite
28	  presentation, universally open, an open map);
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` (`Albanese/RationalMapProd.lean`) — pair
30	  `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂` into `X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G`;
31	
32	and then composes on the right with the **group-object difference morphism**
33	`GrpObj.diff G = (g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G`, whose underlying scheme morphism
34	`(GrpObj.diff G).left : G ×_{k̄} G ⟶ G` is the honest group law `m ∘ (id × inv)`.
35	
36	The pairing `prod` requires the source `X ×_{k̄} X` to be integral; this is passed
37	as the hypothesis `[IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)]`. The caller supplies it via
38	the project lemma `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` (self-product is smooth over
39	`k̄`, hence reduced) together with `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace` — the
40	`isIntegral_pullback_self` recipe is spelled out in the module note below and lives
41	in `CodimOneExtension.lean`'s cone (it depends on the standard-smooth chart machinery
42	there, so it is not restated here to keep this file a Mathlib-only leaf).
43	
44	## Main definitions
45	
46	* `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.diff` — the division morphism `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` of a group
47	  object in any cartesian-monoidal category.
48	* `AlgebraicGeometry.grpObjDiffLeft` — its underlying scheme morphism, typed as
49	  `pullback G.hom G.hom ⟶ G.left`.
50	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap` — Milne's
51	  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`.
52	
53	## Main results
54	
55	* `AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self` / `..._snd_self` — the two
56	  projections of the self-product are open maps.
57	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap_compHom_over` — the
58	  difference map is a `k̄`-rational map (`Φ.compHom G.hom = (pr₁ ≫ X.hom).toRat`).
59	
60	Sub-steps 2 (`(x, x) ∈ Dom Φ ↔ x ∈ Dom f`) and 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound)
61	remain; they consume `differenceRationalMap` built here.
62	-/
63	
64	set_option autoImplicit false
65	
66	universe u
67	
68	open CategoryTheory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonoidalCategory Limits TopologicalSpace
69	open scoped CategoryTheory.MonObj
70	
71	namespace CategoryTheory.GrpObj
72	
73	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
74	
75	/-- **Difference / division morphism of a group object.** For a group object `G`
76	in a cartesian-monoidal category, `GrpObj.diff G = fst / snd : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` is the
77	morphism `(g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹`, i.e. the group law composed with inversion on the
78	second factor. There is no ready-made such morphism in mathlib (`GrpObj.conj` and
79	`GrpObj.commutator` are the only prebuilt `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` maps), so we build it from
80	the hom-set group structure `CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`. -/
81	noncomputable def diff (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ⟶ G :=
82	  fst G G / snd G G
83	
84	end CategoryTheory.GrpObj
85	
86	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
87	
88	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
89	
90	/-- The underlying scheme morphism `(GrpObj.diff G).left : G ×_{k̄} G ⟶ G` of the
91	group-object difference morphism, typed directly against `pullback G.hom G.hom`
92	(definitionally `(G ⊗ G).left` via `Over.tensorObj_left`). This coercion avoids the
93	`(G ⊗ G).left` vs `pullback G.hom G.hom` transparency wall when right-composing a
94	rational map with target `pullback G.hom G.hom`. -/
95	noncomputable def grpObjDiffLeft (G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [GrpObj G] :
96	    pullback G.hom G.hom ⟶ G.left :=
97	  (GrpObj.diff G).left
98	
99	/-- The difference morphism sits over `Spec k̄`: `grpObjDiffLeft G ≫ G.hom =
100	pr₁ ≫ G.hom`. This is the reduction that turns `Φ.compHom G.hom` into the structure
101	morphism of `X ×_{k̄} X`; it is `Over.w` of `GrpObj.diff G`. -/
102	lemma grpObjDiffLeft_comp_hom (G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [GrpObj G] :
103	    grpObjDiffLeft G ≫ G.hom = pullback.fst G.hom G.hom ≫ G.hom :=
104	  Over.w (GrpObj.diff G)
105	
106	/-- The first projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map (it is smooth, being a
107	base change of the smooth structure morphism, hence universally open). -/
108	theorem isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
109	    IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base :=
110	  (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap
111	
112	/-- The second projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map. -/
113	theorem isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
114	    IsOpenMap (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).base :=
115	  (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap
116	
117	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
118	
119	/-- **Right-composition with a morphism only enlarges the domain of definition.**
120	For a rational map `F : X ⤏ Y` and a morphism `g : Y ⟶ Z`, every point at which
121	`F` is defined is also a point at which `F.compHom g` is defined: a partial-map
122	representative `F₀` of `F` yields the representative `F₀.compHom g` of
123	`F.compHom g` with the *same* domain (post-composing with a total morphism never
124	destroys definedness). This is Milne Lemma 3.3, Sub-step 1: the difference map
125	`Φ = (prod ⋯).compHom (diff_G)` is defined wherever the pairing `prod ⋯` is. -/
126	theorem le_domain_compHom {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.RationalMap Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
127	    F.domain ≤ (F.compHom g).domain := by
128	  intro x hx
129	  rw [RationalMap.mem_domain] at hx ⊢
130	  obtain ⟨F₀, hxF₀, hF₀⟩ := hx
131	  exact ⟨F₀.compHom g, hxF₀, by rw [RationalMap.compHom_toRationalMap, hF₀]⟩
132	
133	variable {X G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
134	  [Smooth X.hom]
135	  [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
136	
137	/-- **Milne's difference rational map** `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`.
138	
139	For a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over `k̄`, with `f` defined
140	over `k̄` (`f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap`), the difference map
141	`Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G` is `((f ∘ pr₁) ×_{k̄} (f ∘ pr₂)) ∘ (g, h ↦ g · h⁻¹)`.
142	
143	The self-product must be integral for the pairing `prod` to be well-defined; this
144	is the hypothesis `[IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)]`. -/
145	noncomputable def differenceRationalMap
146	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
147	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
148	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] :
149	    (pullback X.hom X.hom).RationalMap G.left :=
150	  (RationalMap.prod (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) G.hom G.hom
151	      (f.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X))
152	      (f.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X))
153	      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap])
154	      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap,
155	        pullback.condition])).compHom (grpObjDiffLeft G)
156	
157	/-- **The difference map is a `k̄`-rational map.** Right-composing `Φ` with the
158	structure morphism `G.hom` recovers the structure morphism `pr₁ ≫ X.hom` of
159	`X ×_{k̄} X`. -/
160	theorem differenceRationalMap_compHom_over
161	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
162	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
163	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] :
164	    (differenceRationalMap f hover).compHom G.hom
165	      = (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap := by
166	  simp only [differenceRationalMap]
167	  rw [RationalMap.compHom_compHom, grpObjDiffLeft_comp_hom]
168	  exact RationalMap.prod_compHom_over _ _ _ _ _ _ _
169	
170	/-- The explicit pairing partial map `U ×_{k̄} U → G ×_{k̄} G` of Milne's
171	construction (`(x, y) ↦ (φ_U(x), φ_U(y))`), built from the maximal representative
172	`φ_U = f.toPartialMap` of `f` over the common domain `Dom(f∘pr₁) ⊓ Dom(f∘pr₂)`. It
173	is the partial-map witness that `prod` is defined on `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f)`. -/
174	noncomputable def precompDiffPairing
175	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
176	    [IsReduced X.left] [G.left.IsSeparated] :
177	    (pullback X.hom X.hom).PartialMap (pullback G.hom G.hom) where
178	  domain := (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
179	      (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).domain
180	    ⊓ (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
181	      (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).domain
182	  dense_domain := by
183	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
184	    exact (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
185	        (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).dense_domain.inter_of_isOpen_left
186	      (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
187	        (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).dense_domain
188	      (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
189	        (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).domain.isOpen
190	  hom := pullback.lift
191	    ((pullback X.hom X.hom).homOfLE inf_le_left
192	      ≫ (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
193	        (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).hom)
194	    ((pullback X.hom X.hom).homOfLE inf_le_right
195	      ≫ (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
196	        (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).hom)
197	    (by
198	      -- Over-`Spec k̄` structure making *both* projections `S`-morphisms (they agree
199	      -- after `X.hom` by `pullback.condition`), so the precomposed legs inherit
200	      -- over-ness from `f.toPartialMap` without ever unfolding `precomp`.
201	      letI : X.left.Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨X.hom⟩
202	      letI : G.left.Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨G.hom⟩
203	      letI : (pullback X.hom X.hom).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
204	        ⟨pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom⟩
205	      haveI : f.IsOver (Spec (.of kbar)) := RationalMap.isOver_iff.mpr hover
206	      haveI : (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).IsOver (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨rfl⟩
207	      haveI : (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).IsOver (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨pullback.condition.symm⟩
208	      have e₁ : (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
209	            (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).hom ≫ G.hom
210	          = (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
211	            (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).domain.ι ≫ (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) :=
212	        (PartialMap.isOver_iff (S := Spec (.of kbar))).mp inferInstance
213	      have e₂ : (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
214	            (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).hom ≫ G.hom
215	          = (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
216	            (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).domain.ι ≫ (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) :=
217	        (PartialMap.isOver_iff (S := Spec (.of kbar))).mp inferInstance
218	      rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, e₁, e₂]
219	      simp only [← Category.assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_ι])
220	
221	omit [GrpObj G] in
222	/-- The explicit pairing partial map represents Milne's `prod (f∘pr₁, f∘pr₂)`. Both
223	are `k̄`-rational maps out of the integral scheme `X ×_{k̄} X`, so it suffices to
224	match their induced morphisms `Spec K(X × X) ⟶ G ×_{k̄} G`: on each `pullback`
225	factor the pairing's generic-point morphism restricts to `(f ∘ prᵢ)`'s. -/
226	theorem precompDiffPairing_toRationalMap
227	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
228	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
229	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] [IsReduced X.left] [G.left.IsSeparated] :
230	    (precompDiffPairing f hover).toRationalMap
231	      = RationalMap.prod (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) G.hom G.hom
232	        (f.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X))
233	        (f.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X))
234	        (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap])
235	        (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap,
236	          pullback.condition]) := by
237	  refine RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ ?_
238	  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, prod_fromFunctionField]
239	  apply pullback.hom_ext
240	  · rw [pullback.lift_fst]
241	    have hrhs : (f.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
242	          (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).fromFunctionField
243	        = (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
244	          (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)).fromFunctionField := by
245	      rw [← RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap,
246	        f.toRationalMap_toPartialMap]
247	    rw [hrhs, ← PartialMap.fromFunctionField_restrict
248	      (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X))
249	      (precompDiffPairing f hover).dense_domain inf_le_left]
250	    simp only [PartialMap.fromFunctionField, PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem,
251	      PartialMap.restrict_hom, Category.assoc]
252	    congr 1
253	    exact pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
254	  · rw [pullback.lift_snd]
255	    have hrhs : (f.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
256	          (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).fromFunctionField
257	        = (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)
258	          (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)).fromFunctionField := by
259	      rw [← RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap,
260	        f.toRationalMap_toPartialMap]
261	    rw [hrhs, ← PartialMap.fromFunctionField_restrict
262	      (f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X))
263	      (precompDiffPairing f hover).dense_domain inf_le_right]
264	    simp only [PartialMap.fromFunctionField, PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem,
265	      PartialMap.restrict_hom, Category.assoc]
266	    congr 1
267	    exact pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
268	
269	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3, Sub-step 1 (domain lower bound / Sub-step 2 easy direction).**
270	The difference rational map `Φ` is defined on `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f)`: every point
271	`p` of `X ×_{k̄} X` projecting into `Dom(f)` under both projections lies in
272	`Dom(Φ)`. This is Milne's "clearly `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)` if `f` is defined at
273	`x`" — indeed at every `(x, y)` with `x, y ∈ Dom(f)` — made precise: a maximal
274	representative `(U, φ_U)` of `f` (`f.toPartialMap`) supplies the honest morphism
275	`U ×_{k̄} U → G` of Milne's construction, which represents `Φ`. -/
276	theorem le_domain_differenceRationalMap
277	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
278	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
279	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] [IsReduced X.left] [G.left.IsSeparated] :
280	    (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain) ⊓ (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain)
281	      ≤ (differenceRationalMap f hover).domain := by
282	  -- Right-composition with `diff_G` only enlarges the domain, reducing to `prod`;
283	  -- the explicit pairing `precompDiffPairing` is a representative defined on all of
284	  -- `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f)`.
285	  refine le_trans ?_ (le_domain_compHom _ (grpObjDiffLeft G))
286	  intro p hp
287	  rw [RationalMap.mem_domain]
288	  exact ⟨precompDiffPairing f hover, hp, precompDiffPairing_toRationalMap f hover⟩
289	
290	end Scheme.RationalMap
291	
292	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.SpreadingOut
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated
11	/-!
12	
13	# Rational maps between schemes
14	
15	## Main definitions
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PartialMap`: A partial map from `X` to `Y` (`X.PartialMap Y`) is
18	  a morphism into `Y` defined on a dense open subscheme of `X`.
19	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PartialMap.equiv`:
20	  Two partial maps are equivalent if they are equal on a dense open subscheme.
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap`:
22	  A rational map from `X` to `Y` (`X ⤏ Y`) is an equivalence class of partial maps.
23	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.equivFunctionFieldOver`:
24	  Given `S`-schemes `X` and `Y` such that `Y` is locally of finite type and `X` is integral,
25	  `S`-morphisms `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` correspond bijectively to `S`-rational maps from `X` to `Y`.
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`:
27	  If `X` is reduced and `Y` is separated, then any `f : X ⤏ Y` can be realized as a partial
28	  map on `f.domain`, the domain of definition of `f`.
29	-/
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory hiding Quotient
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme.{u}} (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S)
40	
41	namespace Scheme
42	
43	/--
44	A partial map from `X` to `Y` (`X.PartialMap Y`) is a morphism into `Y`
45	defined on a dense open subscheme of `X`.
46	-/
47	structure PartialMap (X Y : Scheme.{u}) where
48	  /-- The domain of definition of a partial map. -/
49	  domain : X.Opens
50	  dense_domain : Dense (domain : Set X)
51	  /-- The underlying morphism of a partial map. -/
52	  hom : ↑domain ⟶ Y
53	
54	variable (S) in
55	/-- A partial map is an `S`-map if the underlying morphism is. -/
56	abbrev PartialMap.IsOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X.PartialMap Y) :=
57	  f.hom.IsOver S
58	
59	namespace PartialMap
60	
61	lemma ext_iff (f g : X.PartialMap Y) :
62	    f = g ↔ ∃ e : f.domain = g.domain, f.hom = (X.isoOfEq e).hom ≫ g.hom := by
63	  constructor
64	  · rintro rfl
65	    simp
66	  · obtain ⟨U, hU, f⟩ := f
67	    obtain ⟨V, hV, g⟩ := g
68	    rintro ⟨rfl : U = V, e⟩
69	    congr 1
70	    simpa using e
71	
72	@[ext]
73	lemma ext (f g : X.PartialMap Y) (e : f.domain = g.domain)
74	    (H : f.hom = (X.isoOfEq e).hom ≫ g.hom) : f = g := by
75	  rw [ext_iff]
76	  exact ⟨e, H⟩
77	
78	/-- The restriction of a partial map to a smaller domain. -/
79	@[simps hom domain]
80	noncomputable
81	def restrict (f : X.PartialMap Y) (U : X.Opens)
82	    (hU : Dense (U : Set X)) (hU' : U ≤ f.domain) : X.PartialMap Y where
83	  domain := U
84	  dense_domain := hU

223	/-- Two partial maps are equivalent if they are equal on a dense open subscheme. -/
224	protected noncomputable
225	def equiv (f g : X.PartialMap Y) : Prop :=
226	  ∃ (W : X.Opens) (hW : Dense (W : Set X)) (hWl : W ≤ f.domain) (hWr : W ≤ g.domain),

230	lemma equivalence_rel : Equivalence (@Scheme.PartialMap.equiv X Y) where
231	  refl f := ⟨f.domain, f.dense_domain, by simp⟩
232	  symm {f g} := by
233	    intro ⟨W, hW, hWl, hWr, e⟩
234	    exact ⟨W, hW, hWr, hWl, e.symm⟩
235	  trans {f g h} := by
236	    intro ⟨W₁, hW₁, hW₁l, hW₁r, e₁⟩ ⟨W₂, hW₂, hW₂l, hW₂r, e₂⟩
237	    refine ⟨W₁ ⊓ W₂, hW₁.inter_of_isOpen_left hW₂ W₁.2, inf_le_left.trans hW₁l,
238	      inf_le_right.trans hW₂r, ?_⟩
239	    dsimp at e₁ e₂
240	    simp only [restrict_domain, restrict_hom, ← X.homOfLE_homOfLE (U := W₁ ⊓ W₂) inf_le_left hW₁l,
241	      Category.assoc, e₁, ← X.homOfLE_homOfLE (U := W₁ ⊓ W₂) inf_le_right hW₂r, ← e₂]
242	    simp only [homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]

253	lemma equiv_of_fromSpecStalkOfMem_eq [IrreducibleSpace X]
254	    {x : X} [X.IsGermInjectiveAt x] (f g : X.PartialMap Y)
255	    (hxf : x ∈ f.domain) (hxg : x ∈ g.domain)
256	    (H : f.fromSpecStalkOfMem hxf = g.fromSpecStalkOfMem hxg) : f.equiv g := by
257	  have hdense : Dense ((f.domain ⊓ g.domain) : Set X) :=
258	    f.dense_domain.inter_of_isOpen_left g.dense_domain f.domain.2
259	  have := (isGermInjectiveAt_iff_of_isOpenImmersion (f := (f.domain ⊓ g.domain).ι)
260	    (x := ⟨x, hxf, hxg⟩)).mp ‹_›
261	  have := spread_out_unique_of_isGermInjective' (X := (f.domain ⊓ g.domain).toScheme)
262	    (X.homOfLE inf_le_left ≫ f.hom) (X.homOfLE inf_le_right ≫ g.hom) (x := ⟨x, hxf, hxg⟩) ?_
263	  · obtain ⟨U, hxU, e⟩ := this
264	    refine ⟨(f.domain ⊓ g.domain).ι ''ᵁ U, ((f.domain ⊓ g.domain).ι ''ᵁ U).2.dense
265	      ⟨_, ⟨_, hxU, rfl⟩⟩,
266	      ((Set.image_subset_range _ _).trans_eq (Subtype.range_val)).trans inf_le_left,
267	      ((Set.image_subset_range _ _).trans_eq (Subtype.range_val)).trans inf_le_right, ?_⟩
268	    rw [← cancel_epi (Scheme.Hom.isoImage _ _).hom]
269	    simp only [restrict_hom, ← Category.assoc] at e ⊢
270	    convert! e using 2 <;> rw [← cancel_mono (Scheme.Opens.ι _)] <;> simp
271	  · rw [← f.fromSpecStalkOfMem_restrict hdense inf_le_left ⟨hxf, hxg⟩,
272	      ← g.fromSpecStalkOfMem_restrict hdense inf_le_right ⟨hxf, hxg⟩] at H
273	    simpa only [fromSpecStalkOfMem, restrict_domain, Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem, Spec.map_inv,
274	      restrict_hom, Category.assoc, IsIso.eq_inv_comp, IsIso.hom_inv_id_assoc] using H
